Jim Lawrence, what's going on? We have split the weigh-in process into two pieces, and that's pretty much how I have facilities. There's kind of like the admin and tech stuff, which is kind of these first few pieces. And then you have your kind of nutrition and finalizing the sale, etc. standpoint, which is kind of the second piece here. That being said, this first video is just going to be the first piece. This is something you have someone else do, because it doesn't really require a lot of skill. It's just basically administrative work. But it's really important to do it. That being said, what I would highly, highly, highly recommend you do is print this page out, laminate it, and then glue it or duct tape it to your counter. You can have it on a sheet on a clipboard if you want to. That's why I had so many of them. But this is one that you're going to use this over and over and over again. And you have to use the checklist, because if you're doing 50 people or 100 people, you're going to forget things unless you have a checklist. And so I just did this every time, and you just get consistent with it, and people always have the same process. All right. I'd like to introduce my lovely assistant Nolan at our— So if you look here, I'm just going to be checking off the boxes just like I normally would. So Nolan comes in, and I'm going to have my stack of contracts. So typically what we'd have is this table set up right at the door of the facility. So Nolan walks in the front door. You can do this at your—put all the contracts that you have in a pile, separate out the ones that are a pain in the ass—I call them PIAs or special snowflakes—but people who've just random things, I've never done this without some sort of issues coming up. So have a special snowflake pile, and then your normal pile. And so the first thing I'm going to do is make sure that Nolan's contract is up to date and all of the billing payments that he had went through. So I'm going to pull his contract. Like Nolan is a nodice, so good for him. And then what I'm going to do is I'm going to go ahead right there, pull up my group and add him. Android's and iPhones are different, but you're going to have him pull up his phone. You're going to click check in, and then you're going to search for your gym. He's probably going to be at your gym already, so it should come up, and then you're going to show him how to do his first check in, and you're going to check in form. He's going to be like, great, please check in. And if you want, you can write something fun on it. But you're like, so every time you work out, you're going to get that you are being accountable, all right? But that way we get check ins from, and that's that. Okay. Now, next thing we're going to do is we're going to go and download the app called Voxer on your phone. So we're going to the app store. We're going to find Voxer. All right? That's the app that we use. We use Voxer because it's free, and you don't have to give that your phone number, which is great. So you're going to download Voxer, and as soon as you download Voxer, you're going to, and usually you can sign up on Voxer via Facebook. So you don't have to create an account. It takes one click, which makes it significantly easier. And then you're going to go to his profile. All right? So when you're on there, you can hit the hamburger on the top left, and you click my profile, and that's going to have his code name. Okay? And you're also going to know your code name. So you just create a new message. You search for your username. You open a new message, and you have him send you one. And that way at the end of the day, you're going to have all these messages from new clients. You're going to be able to interact with Vox, being like, hey, it was awesome meeting you, blah, blah, blah. And if you want, you can take a picture of him so that you can make sure you know who it is, because sometimes it gets annoying. All right? So, boom, we got him to download Voxer. Boom, we got him to send the first message. Voxer pulled up, be like, hey, you're going to be Anthony. It's going to be Jesus. It's going to be whoever. Right? And so then I'm going to find them using the exact same thing and open a message there. That way, his first two messages are going to be me and his coach. That way, he always knows if he needs anything, you can always reach out to us. And for some reason, Anthony, as I get back to you, let me know so I can holler down as thoracic. And the next thing is you're setting expectations. So I know that, as we already talked about how we're going to be keeping in touch with every day, I'm going to be asking you questions about your fitness some days, some days when we ask about your nutrition, some days we're just asking you how you feel, how you sleep and how's your energy. All right? It's your life, but I'm not going to batter you every day with 20 questions because you're probably not going to like that. All right? So we spread them out throughout the week so that it kind of makes it easy for you. All I ask is that you remember that that is part of the guarantee, which should be on here, that you just need to respond to me so that I can help you. All right? Fair enough? Great. So, boom, I set the expectations. And usually, we'll respond by end of the day, but we're usually a little better than that. But sometimes if I'm on the floor or I have like back-to-back going, fair enough? Great. All right? Next thing is, depends on how you run your gym. All right? So if you have workout scheduling, like if you use MindBody or use Zimplanner, there's an app for them to schedule. So you can download that app for them and show them how to schedule their first session. And you're going to show them how to do it so that they know how to do it. And then you can have them actually schedule the next session, which kind of kills two birds because they always should know when the next time they come into the facility. Sessions and like you'll have any capacity issues and they can come whatever. And it's like, cool. So here's our explain and 80-20 soft commitment. So what that is is saying, listen, I'm not telling you have to come to this time, but I'm telling you that this is the best time that you'll get the most personalized attention. So you always want to do it in reverse order of the most packed time. So it's like, I want you to come on Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday. So always start on Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday. Otherwise everyone wants to do Monday, Wednesday, Friday. So you're like, you're going to come Tuesday, Thursday, Saturday at 7 a.m. because that's the least time. All right? If he says yes, then fantastic. That's when he's coming. All right? And then I literally go in reverse order because I can tell him those are the times you can get the most personalized attention. Right? And you go in reverse order. Otherwise, everyone's coming at 5.30 Wednesday, Wednesday, Friday. And it's paying the, we actually can get him the most personalized attention.
